data.title: Broadway Industrial Group Appoints Group CFO
data.description:
Broadway Industrial Group redesignated Tiong Chi Sieng from chief financial officer to group CFO, effective Feb. 21, according to a same-day filing with the Singapore Exchange.

He will be responsible for the finance and treasury function of the group, the filing added.

Alle Zeitangaben in Central European Time (CET)

SATURDAY FEBRUARY 22, 2025

Israeli hostages to be released, with Palestinian prisoners exchanged in return - The remaining six living Israeli hostages held by Hamas are due to be released today under the terms of the phase one agreement between Hamas and Israel, with more Palestinian prisoners released in exchange.

Trump to speak at CPAC - U.S. President Donald Trump is expected to address conservatives gathered near Washington D.C. for the annual Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC).

15:00 - Germany's Scholz holds final rally ahead of Sunday's general election - German Chancellor and Social Democrats (SPD) frontrunner Olaf Scholz speaks at final rally in Potsdam, his constituency outside Berlin.

15:00 - German chancellor-hopeful Merz and CSU chief Soeder hold final rally in Munich beer hall. - German opposition conservative CDU chancellor-hopeful Friedrich Merz holds his final rally in a Munich beer hall, assisted by Bavarian state premier Markus Soeder, before Germans elect a new parliament on Sunday.

20:15 - Germany’s Scholz, chancellor candidates Merz, Habeck and Weidel in final TV debate before election. - German broadcasters Pro7 and Sat1 host Chancellor Olaf Scholz for a live debate with his conservative challenger Friedrich Merz, Economy Minister Robert Habeck of the Greens and opposition far-right AfD co-leader Alice Weidel. It will be the last TV debate before the snap election on Sunday.

SUNDAY FEBRUARY 23, 2025

Germany holds snap elections - Germany will hold an early general election after Chancellor Olaf Scholz’s three-way coalition collapsed in late 2024 over a budget dispute.

18:00 - Officials count votes in general election - Volunteers gather to count votes in Germany's general election

20:15 - Party leaders attend TV debate after exit polls in Berlin

MONDAY FEBRUARY 24, 2025

The main German parties, SPD, CDU/CSU, AfD and the Greens react to the results of a snap election.

Zelenskiy addresses the nation on the 3rd anniversary of Russian invasion - Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelenskiy issues video address to the nation to mark the 3rd anniversary of Russian full-scale invasion.

French President Emmanuel Macron visits Washington D.C. and meets with U.S. President Donald Trump.

Foreign ministers meet in Brussels for EU-Israel Association Council and Foreign Affairs Council - EU foreign ministers gather in Brussels for EU-Israel Association Council and Foreign Affairs Council. Israeli Foreign minister Gideon Sa'ar is expected to participate.

G20 finance and central bank deputies meeting - South Africa will host a meeting of the G20 finance and central bank deputies on February 24 - 25 in Cape Town.

09:00 - UN Human Rights Council opens in Geneva - United Nations Human Rights Council opens the first of its three sessions of the year in Geneva, with statement from U.N. chief Antonio Guterres and U.N. High Commissioner for human rights, Volker Turk.

TUESDAY FEBRUARY 25, 2025

German court holds hearing against four suspected Hamas members - A court in Berlin will hold a hearing against four men accused of acting as foreign operatives for the Hamas militant group in Europe.

G20 finance and central bank deputies meeting - South Africa will host a meeting of the G20 finance and central bank deputies on February 24 - 25 in Cape Town.

WEDNESDAY FEBRUARY 26, 2025

G20 finance ministers and central bank governors meeting - South Africa will host the first meeting of the G20 finance ministers and central bank governors on February 26 - 27 in Cape Town.

THURSDAY FEBRUARY 27, 2025

French President Macron visits Lisbon, addresses Portuguese parliament

Senate panel to vote on Trump's DOJ antitrust pick - The U.S. Senate judiciary committee is scheduled to vote on President Donald Trump's pick to oversee the U.S. Department of Justice's blockbuster antitrust litigation against Apple and Alphabet's Google.

G20 finance ministers and central bank governors meeting - South Africa will host the first meeting of the G20 finance ministers and central bank governors on February 26 - 27 in Cape Town.

11:11 - German 'Weiberfastnacht' or Women's carnival kicks off in Cologne

FRIDAY FEBRUARY 28, 2025

12:00 - South Africa's Absa hosts event on role of industry, service sectors in driving economic growth - South African lender Absa holds an event on the role of the industry and service sectors in driving economic growth. Trade minister Parks Tau is expected to speak.

SATURDAY MARCH 1, 2025

16:30 - Uruguay shifts to center-left as Orsi takes office - Uruguay is set for a political shift to the center-left on March 1 as Yamandu Orsi takes office as the country's next president. Orsi, a 57-year-old former mayor backed by leftist ex-President Jose "Pepe" Mujica, narrowly won the November election against the ruling center-right coalition.

SUNDAY MARCH 2, 2025

No events scheduled

data.title: Ampol Constructive on Refining Margins Normalizing, CEO Says
Ampol CEO: Expects Refining Margins to Bounce Around Long-Term Average
Ampol CEO: Encouraged by Chinese Oil Product Exports Remaining Stable
Ampol Confident That Refining Margins Will Continue to Rebound — Interview
data.description:
(04:30 GMT) *Ampol Constructive on Refining Margins Normalizing, CEO Says
(04:30 GMT) *Ampol CEO: Expects Refining Margins to Bounce Around Long-Term Average
(04:30 GMT) *Ampol CEO: Encouraged by Chinese Oil Product Exports Remaining Stable
(04:31 GMT) *Ampol CEO: U.S. Tariffs on Canada, Mexico Would Likely Drive Up Product Cracks
(04:31 GMT) Ampol Confident That Refining Margins Will Continue to Rebound — Interview

By David Winning

SYDNEY--Ampol is confident that refining margins will continue to recover after hitting a trough in the third quarter of last year, helped by lower interest rates stimulating demand for fuel and Chinese product exports remaining stable.

In an interview, Ampol Chief Executive Matt Halliday said it is encouraging that China hasn't flooded the market with surplus oil products despite ongoing weakness in its economy led by a soft property sector. Meanwhile, supply elsewhere is tightening at a time when fuel demand continues to grow, he said.

"We're pretty constructive on margins normalizing," Halliday said. "We expect them to bounce around the long-term average."

The Australia-based oil refiner and marketer on Monday reported a 68% decline in its 2024 profit to 234.8 million Australian dollars, equivalent to US$149.3 million, when measured on a replacement cost-of-sales basis, which strips out the impact of movements in oil prices by restating the cost of sales using the replacement cost of goods sold. Its statutory net profit dropped by 78%.

Much of the earnings decline stemmed from refining margins worsening in the second half of the year. Ampol's Lytton Refiner Margin averaged US$7.08 a barrel in 2024, down 45% on a year earlier. But it reached a trough of US$1.48 a barrel in the third quarter when Ampol carried out maintenance work at its Lytton plant in eastern Australia and markets were awash with oil products.

Ampol said the material deterioration in global refining margins and the weaker operational performance of the Lytton plant meant its second-half earnings before interest and tax were some A$500 million lower than the same period of 2023.

Still, Halliday signaled the outlook is improving. The Lytton Refiner Margin was US$6.31 a barrel in January, improving from December but below historical averages. During February, Singapore product cracks strengthened by another US$2 per barrel.

Refining margins have responded to steps taken by the U.S. in the waning days of the Biden administration to target Russia's so-called shadow fleet that transports illicit oil, Halliday said. The Trump administration hasn't dialed back on those sanctions.

The illicit tanker fleet sprang up to evade Western sanctions on Russian oil, allowing Moscow to move crude to buyers in India and China by flagging ships in countries such as Gabon.

"Tightening of sanctions made life harder for Chinese and Indian refiners and that started to reset supply chains as well," Halliday said.

Ampol acknowledged that speculation over trade policy and shifts in global political dynamics is stoking more uncertainty in markets, with the U.S. not yet removing the threat of 25% tariffs on imports from Canada and Mexico entirely. Still, it is too early to conclude what the impact of this uncertainty will be, the company said.

"What we know is that Mexico and Canada are really important supply chains into the U.S. refinery sector," Halliday said. Tariffs on those countries "would reset supply chains and drive up volatility and product cracks. That would be our expectation."

Write to David Winning at david.winning@wsj.com

data.title: Ampol CEO: U.S. Tariffs on Canada, Mexico Would Likely Drive Up Product Cracks
RailTel Corporation of India Bags New Order Worth INR3 Billion
data.description:
RailTel Corporation of India has received a new work order worth 2.88 billion Indian rupees from the East Central Railway, India, according to a Friday filing with the Indian stock exchanges.

The contract provision of Kavach, an indigenous train collision avoidance system, on a low-density railway track in 502.2 RKm of East Central Railway.

The contract is to be executed by Feb. 20, 2027.

Shares of the company were up over 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Malaysia's Consumer Inflation May Accelerate to 2.6% This Year — Market Talk
data.description: Malaysia's consumer inflation could rise to 2.6% this year from 1.8% in 2024, due to the gradual withdrawal of the RON95 fuel subsidy from mid-year, Apex Securities analyst Afham Zulghafir says in a note. Sales tax expansion and likely rise in consumer demand due to rising household incomes could also push prices higher, he says. The country's GDP growth could remain strong at 4.6% in 2025, compared with 5.1% last year, supported by resilient domestic demand and investment spending, he reckons. With CPI remaining within the official target range of 2.0%-3.5%, the analyst expects Bank Negara to maintain the policy rate at 3.0% in 2025, supporting Malaysia's economic growth.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0422 GMT - Malaysia's consumer inflation could rise to 2.6% this year from 1.8% in 2024, due to the gradual withdrawal of the RON95 fuel subsidy from mid-year, Apex Securities analyst Afham Zulghafir says in a note. Sales tax expansion and likely rise in consumer demand due to rising household incomes could also push prices higher, he says. The country's GDP growth could remain strong at 4.6% in 2025, compared with 5.1% last year, supported by resilient domestic demand and investment spending, he reckons. With CPI remaining within the official target range of 2.0%-3.5%, the analyst expects Bank Negara to maintain the policy rate at 3.0% in 2025, supporting Malaysia's economic growth.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0411 GMT - China's official manufacturing PMI likely indicated another contraction in February with a 49.8 reading, Moody's Analytics says in a note. "Even as production chugs along to fulfill existing orders, a further 10% tariff in the U.S. on goods from China should hit new orders," it says. China's official gauge of factory activity contracted to 49.1 in January from December's 50.1 as factories suspended operations days ahead of the Lunar New Year holiday. As for the non-manufacturing PMI, retail sales should get a boost from Lunar New Year activity but construction will likely stay in the doldrums, it says. China's manufacturing PMI data is due Saturday. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

0338 GMT - The Singapore dollar strengthens against its U.S. counterpart in the Asian session, driven by risk-on sentiment fueled by gains in U.S. stock-index futures and projected results from Germany's election. Initial estimates showed the CDU and its CSU sister party securing just under 29% of the vote, followed by Alternative for Germany, or AfD, with around 20%. Given that the election outcome aligns with expectations, analysts at Maybank say there are hopes of reduced political uncertainty. Additionally, signs of fading U.S. exceptionalism have emerged, supported by recent economic data. USD/SGD is 0.3% lower at 1.3319. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0222 GMT - More upside for JGB yields and the yen looks likely, Capital Economics' John Higgins says in a commentary. Japanese inflation and PMI data released Friday bolster Capital Economics' view that the JGB 10-year yield will continue to rise, the chief markets economist says. The ongoing rise in JGB yields probably stems from investors' reassessment of the Bank of Japan's monetary-policy outlook, Higgins says. The BOJ's policy rate is likely to reach 1.25% by end-2026, which feeds into Capital Economics' forecast for JGB 10-year yield to end 2025 at 1.75%. This could also buoy the yen against the dollar, says Capital Economics, which forecasts USD/JPY to reach 145 by end-2025. USD/JPY is 0.1% higher at 149.46. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0219 GMT - Singapore's CPI likely rose 1.95% on year in January, according to the median estimate of six economists polled by The Wall Street Journal. That would compare with December's 1.6% increase. Headline inflation is expected to pick up due to a higher contribution from accommodation costs and private road transport, Sanjay Mathur, chief economist for Southeast Asia and India at ANZ, says in a note. Core inflation is projected to have eased to 1.6% last month from December's 1.8% rise, according to five of the economists polled. Core inflation will likely continue declining, partly due to last year's higher base from the GST hike, ANZ says. The data are due Monday. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0123 GMT - Asian currencies strengthen against the U.S. dollar in early trade amid prospects of Fed rate cuts that would decrease the allure of U.S. fixed-income assets and demand for the greenback. U.S. economic data released last Friday were disappointing, NAB's Rodrigo Catril says in commentary. Also, "playing to the emerging theme of a loss of U.S. exceptionalism, other services PMIs around the globe showed improvements," the senior FX strategist says. The weak U.S. data led to overnight-index-swap market bringing forward a 25 bp rate cut by the Fed to July from September, Catril adds. USD/KRW falls 0.2% to 1,431.80; AUD/USD edges 0.1% higher to 0.6369. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0035 GMT - Euro rises against other currencies in the early Asian session as traders digest the outcome of Germany's election. Exit polls and early vote-counting suggest the Christian Democratic Union won around 29% of votes and will probably lead a coalition government, CBA's Global Economic & Markets Research team say in a research report. "However, it is unclear whether two or three parties will be needed to form a coalition government," the team says. Market participants will likely be closely watching whether the eventual government agrees to reform the 'debt brake' to support economic growth, the team adds. EUR/USD rises 0.3% to 1.0499; EUR/JPY gains 0.2% to 156.42. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0025 GMT - South Korea's benchmark Kospi falls 0.6% to 2638.50 in early trade as semiconductor and steel stocks retreat. Foreign and institutional investors are net sellers, as Wall Street's Friday falls are weighing on sentiment. Memory-chip makers Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix decline 1.4% and 3.3%, respectively. Chip-making equipment supplier Hanmi Semiconductor loses 4.6%. Steelmaker Dongguk Steel slides 3.1%, paring Friday's sharp gains fueled by Seoul's move to impose anti-dumping duties on Chinese steel products. USD/KRW is 0.2% higher at 1,436.50 in Seoul onshore trading. South Korea's 10-year government bond yield is down 3.2 bps at 2.856%. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: Turkey - Factors To Watch on Feb 24
data.description:
Here are news, reports and events that may affect Turkish financial markets on Monday.

The lira traded at 36.4500 against the U.S. dollar at 0327 GMT, unchanged from its close on Friday.

The main BIST 100 share index closed 2.09% lower at 9,602.16 points on Friday.

GLOBAL MARKETS

European shares and the euro climbed on Monday as Germany's election produced no nasty surprises, while Wall Street futures firmed on hopes results from AI diva Nvidia this week would justify the tech sector's sky-high valuations.

CABINET MEETING

President Tayyip Erdogan will chair a meeting of the cabinet of ministers (1200 GMT) and then make a press statement on the meeting.

LAVROV VISIT

Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov and his Turkish counterpart, Hakan Fidan, hold a press conference during Lavrov's visit to Ankara (0800 GMT). They will discuss Russia's recent talks with U.S. officials on ending the war in Ukraine, and how Ankara can contribute to the process, a Turkish foreign ministry source said. Lavrov will also meet Erdogan. (1100 GMT)

Speaking at a press conference in Kyiv, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy said on Sunday he saw Turkey as an important security guarantor for Ukraine.

MANUFACTURING CONFIDENCE (TRGRWT=ECI)

The central bank will release manufacturing confidence data for February. (0700 GMT)

CAPACITY USAGE DATA (TRCAPU=ECI)

The central bank will also publish capacity utilisation data for February. (0700 GMT)

BUSINESS GROUP

President Erdogan on Sunday accused Turkey's business community of attempting to revive a corrupt opposition mindset and compensating their losses through the state treasury. Leaders of TUSIAD, the country's largest business association, already face a formal investigation by authorities.

AK PARTY

At its eighth congress in Ankara on Sunday, Erdogan's ruling AK Party added several names to its Central Decision and Management Board (MKYK) including Serap Yazici Ozbudun, an MP who recently resigned from the small opposition Future Party to join AKP, and former Real Madrid soccer player Mesut Ozil.

ANOTHER MAYOR PROBE

The Istanbul chief prosecutor's office launched an investigation into the city's mayor, Ekrem Imamoglu of the main opposition Republican People's Party (CHP), on charges of "forgery of an official document" over allegations that his university diploma is fake and a Council of Higher Education report. Imamoglu - a probable future presidential challenger to Erdogan who already faces numerous probes and changes that his CHP calls politically motivated - is to testify on February 26, media including DHA reported on Saturday. The CHP said the moves reveal the government's fear of Imamoglu and the electorate.

IRAQI OIL EXPORTS

Iraq will export 185,000 barrels per day from Kurdistan's oilfields through the Iraq-Turkey pipeline once the oil shipments resume, an Iraqi oil ministry official said on Sunday.

Basim Mohammed, deputy oil minister for upstream operations, told Iraq's state news agency that Iraq had reached out to Turkey to confirm the readiness of an export pipeline to the Turkish Mediterranean port of Ceyhan and is expecting a response within 24 hours.

OPPOSITION CANDIDATE

The CHP accepted Imamoglu's application to participate in the party's internal process to select a presidential candidate. The party will hold its initial vote on a candidate in a month.

PKK

State-run Anadolu news agency reported that authorities arrested 30 people suspected of involvement with the outlawed Kurdistan Workers' Party (PKK). The defence ministry said separately on Sunday that its forces "neutralised" six PKK members in Syria and two in Iraq, using a term that usually means killed.

For other related news, double-click on:

Turkish politics (TR-POL)

Turkish equities (TR-E)

Turkish money (TR-M)

Turkish debt (TR-D)

Turkish hot stocks (TR-HOT)

Forex news (FRX)

All emerging market news (EMRG)

All Turkish news

For real-time quotes, double click on:

Istanbul National-100 stock index , interbank lira trading (IYIX=), lira bond trading (0#TRTSYSUM=IS)

data.title: Bank of Canada Deputy Governor Gravelle speaking Monday
data.description:
1315 GMT / 0815 US Eastern time:

  • Panel discussion by Toni Gravelle, Bank of Canada Deputy Governor at the Bank of England Annual Research Conference:
  • Topic is Managing the Central Bank’s Balance Sheet in a Period of Quantitative Tightening

I'm not sure we'll be getting too much from this. Check thisout from the promo site for the conference:

  • There will be no media availability for this event.
  • There will be no audience Q&A period.
  • There will be no Bank of Canada webcast for this event.

However, there will be a Bank of England live stream for media. In order to gain access its necessary to email full name, job title and company to eventsmanagement@bankofengland.co.uk

BoC's Gravelle

data.title: Raffles Medical's Profit Up 4% in H2 2024; Shares Rise 6%
data.description:
Raffles Medical's attributable profit rose 4.3% in the second half of 2024 to SG$31.6 million from SG$30.3 million a year earlier, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Monday.

Earnings per share climbed to SG$0.017 from SG$0.0162 in the comparable period.

Revenue was up 15% year over year to SG$385.9 million from SG$336.2 million, mainly due to improved performance from the hospital services division.

The group declared a final dividend of SG$0.025 per share.

Shares of the medical services company were up over 6% in recent trading.

data.title: Trans-China Automotive Trims Loss in H2 2024; Shares Up 4%
data.description:
Trans-China Automotive's loss for the second half of 2024 by 62% to 29.0 million yuan from 76.2 million yuan a year earlier, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Monday.

Loss per share came in at 0.05 yuan, compared with 0.13 yuan in the comparable period.

Meanwhile, revenue declined 32% year over year to 1.20 billion yuan from 1.77 billion yuan, as automobile sales declined during the period.

Shares of the automobile seller were up over 5% in recent trading.

data.title: Mitsubishi and ENEOS to conduct Front End Engineering Design for Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) Production at the Wakayama Refinery
data.description:
TOKYO, Feb 24, 2025 - (JCN Newswire) - ENEOS Corporation and Mitsubishi Corporation have agreed to jointly conduct a Front End Engineering Design to advance the study of Sustainable Aviation Fuel ("SAF") production facility at the ENEOS Wakayama Plant in Arida City, Japan (the "FEED").

ENEOS has been studying the feasibility of such a facility since 2022. To advance this study to the next stage, ENEOS and MC, who have been jointly assessing the feasibility on the commercialization of SAF, will work together on the FEED. ENEOS has an established sales network, manufacturing and refining technologies and the expertise in feedstock procurement, while MC has expertise in the procurement of feedstock in Japan and around the world. By combining each companies' respective strengths, ENEOS and MC will accelerate the study with the aim of developing large-scale production of SAF in Japan.

The facility, subject to the result of the FEED, is expected to produce approximately 300,000 tons (400,000 KL) of SAF per year from FY2028 onwards, together with bio-naphtha and bio-diesel fuel fractions as byproducts. Its feedstock will include used cooking oil, animal fat, and other wastes and byproducts.

ENEOS and MC place a high priority on addressing climate change issues, and both companies are actively promoting initiatives to reduce CO2 emissions with the aim of helping to achieve a decarbonized, circular society. As part of this effort, the companies will move forward with their study on SAF.

Reference:

(Released on February 21, 2025) ENEOS Selected for the "Support Project for Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) Production and Supply Chain Development": www.eneos.co.jp/english/newsrelease/2024/pdf/20250221_02.pdf

(Released on April 18, 2022) Joint Feasibility Study on the Commercialization ofSustainable Aviation Fuel: www.mitsubishicorp.com/jp/en/news/release/2022/0000048990.html

data.title: Statement on Dan Bongino Being Named Deputy Director of the FBI
data.description:
LONGBOAT KEY, Fla., Feb. 23, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Rumble (NASDAQ:RUM), the video-sharing platform and cloud services provider, today released the following statement from CEO and Chairman Chris Pavlovski regarding the recent appointment of content creator Dan Bongino as Deputy Director of the FBI in the Trump Administration:

“Dan Bongino is a pioneer and a free speech warrior, and it has been an honor to host his content on Rumble. More than just that, he has been a mentor to me personally through these important years of Rumble’s explosive growth, and he has become a dear friend I will have for life. Dan was one of the first major creators to move his content to Rumble in 2020 and has set many livestreaming records ever since. He believes strongly in the innate human right to self-expression—in fact, he’s turned it into an art form—and he knows that the best antidote to speech you don’t like is more speech. We wish Dan all the success in the world as he goes to work for the people of the United States at the request of President Trump, and I commend the President on his selection. Dan Bongino is the best!”

The presence of The Dan Bongino Show on Rumble has been instrumental to the rapid growth of the platform, and the new viewers he brought in have positioned Rumble to continue to increase its user base in the years to come. Bongino is a significant shareholder in the company, and Rumble looks forward to his return when this next phase of his service to his country concludes.

As a content creator on Rumble, Bongino set new standards for streaming excellence, routinely leading all online livestreams in audience size and concurrent viewers, regardless of platform. Given the third-party advertising arrangement related to Bongino's content, the revenue Rumble derived from the content is not material to the company.

ABOUT RUMBLE

Rumble is a high-growth video platform and cloud services provider that is creating an independent infrastructure. Rumble’s mission is to restore the internet to its roots by making it free and open once again. For more information, visit: corp.rumble.com.

Forward-Looking Statements

Certain statements in this press release constitute “forward-looking statements” within the meaning of the U.S. Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. Statements contained in this press release that are not historical facts are forward-looking statements and include, for example, statements regarding our expectations or beliefs regarding the impact of the departure of Dan Bongino on Rumble. Certain of these forward-looking statements can be identified by using words such as “anticipates,” “believes,” “intends,” “estimates,” “targets,” “expects,” “endeavors,” “forecasts,” “well underway,” “could,” “will,” “may,” “future,” “likely,” “on track to deliver,” “on a trajectory,” “continues to,” “looks forward to,” “is primed to,” “plans,” “projects,” “assumes,” “should” or other similar expressions. Such forward-looking statements involve known and unknown risks and uncertainties, and our actual results could differ materially from future results expressed or implied in these forward-looking statements. The forward-looking statements included in this release are based on our current beliefs and expectations of our management as of the date of this release. These statements are not guarantees or indicative of future performance. Important assumptions and other important factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from those forward-looking statements include risks relating to the departure of Dan Bongino on Rumble’s business, including any impact on our monthly active users; the risk that we may be unable to derive additional benefits from Rumble’s relationship with Tether; risks inherent with our increasing affiliation with crypto assets, including volatility, as well as regulatory and reputational risks; the risks of implementing a new treasury diversification strategy; our ability to grow and manage future growth profitably over time, maintain relationships with customers, compete within our industry and retain key employees; the possibility that we may be adversely impacted by economic, business, and/or competitive factors; our limited operating history makes it difficult to evaluate our business and prospects; our recent and rapid growth may not be indicative of future performance; we may not continue to grow or maintain our active user base, and may not be able to achieve or maintain profitability; risks relating to our ability to attract new advertisers, or the potential loss of existing advertisers or the reduction of or failure by existing advertisers to maintain or increase their advertising budgets; Rumble Cloud, our recently launched cloud services business, may not achieve success and, as a result, our business, financial condition and results of operations could be adversely affected; negative media campaigns may adversely impact our financial performance, results of operations, and relationships with our business partners, including content creators and advertisers; spam activity, including inauthentic and fraudulent user activity, if undetected, may contribute, from time to time, to some amount of overstatement of our performance indicators; we collect, store, and process large amounts of user video content and personal information of our users and subscribers and, if our security measures are breached, our sites and applications may be perceived as not being secure, traffic and advertisers may curtail or stop viewing our content or using our services, our business and operating results could be harmed, and we could face governmental investigations and legal claims from users and subscribers; we may fail to comply with applicable privacy laws; we are subject to cybersecurity risks and interruptions or failures in our information technology systems and, notwithstanding our efforts to enhance our protection from such risks, a cyber incident could occur and result in information theft, data corruption, operational disruption and/or financial loss; we may be found to have infringed on the intellectual property of others, which could expose us to substantial losses or restrict our operations; we may face liability for hosting a variety of tortious or unlawful materials uploaded by third parties, notwithstanding the liability protections of Section 230 of the Communications Decency Act of 1996; we may face negative publicity for removing, or declining to remove, certain content, regardless of whether such content violated any law; paid endorsements by our content creators may expose us to regulatory risk, liability, and compliance costs, and, as a result, may adversely affect our business, financial condition and results of operations; our traffic growth, engagement, and monetization depend upon effective operation within and compatibility with operating systems, networks, devices, web browsers and standards, including mobile operating systems, networks, and standards that we do not control; our business depends on continued and unimpeded access to our content and services on the internet and, if we or those who engage with our content experience disruptions in internet service, or if internet service providers are able to block, degrade or charge for access to our content and services, we could incur additional expenses and the loss of traffic and advertisers; we face significant market competition, and if we are unable to compete effectively with our competitors for traffic and advertising spend, our business and operating results could be harmed; we rely on data from third parties to calculate certain of our performance metrics and real or perceived inaccuracies in such metrics may harm our reputation and negatively affect our business; changes to our existing content and services could fail to attract traffic and advertisers or fail to generate revenue; we derive the majority of our revenue from advertising and the failure to attract new advertisers, the loss of existing advertisers, or the reduction of or failure by existing advertisers to maintain or increase their advertising budgets would adversely affect our business; we depend on third-party vendors, including internet service providers, advertising networks, and data centers, to provide core services; hosting and delivery costs may increase unexpectedly; we have offered and intend to continue to offer incentives, including economic incentives, to content creators to join our platform, and these arrangements may involve fixed payment obligations that are not contingent on actual revenue or performance metrics generated by the applicable content creator but rather are based on our modeled financial projections for that creator, which if not satisfied may adversely impact our financial performance, results of operations and liquidity; we may be unable to develop or maintain effective internal controls; potential diversion of management’s attention and consumption of resources as a result of acquisitions of other companies and success in integrating and otherwise achieving the benefits of recent and potential acquisitions; we may fail to maintain adequate operational and financial resources or raise additional capital or generate sufficient cash flows; changes in tax rates, changes in tax treatment of companies engaged in e-commerce, the adoption of new tax legislation, or exposure to additional tax liabilities may adversely impact our financial results; compliance obligations imposed by new privacy laws, laws regulating social media platforms and online speech in certain jurisdictions in which we operate, or industry practices may adversely affect our business; and those additional risks, uncertainties and factors described in more detail under the caption “Risk Factors” in our Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2023, and in our other filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the “SEC”). We do not intend, and, except as required by law, we undertake no obligation, to update any of our forward-looking statements after the issuance of this release to reflect any future events or circumstances. Given these risks and uncertainties, readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on such forward-looking statements.

Rumble on Social Media

Investors and others should note that we announce material financial and operational information to our investors using our investor relations website (investors.rumble.com), press releases, SEC filings and public conference calls and webcasts. We also intend to use certain social media accounts as a means of disclosing information about us and our services and for complying with our disclosure obligations under Regulation FD: the @rumblevideo X (formerly Twitter) account (x.com/rumblevideo), the @gamingonrumble X (formerly Twitter) account (x.com/gamingonrumble), the @rumble TRUTH Social account (truthsocial.com/@rumble), the @chrispavlovski X (formerly Twitter) account (x.com/chrispavlovski), and the @chris TRUTH Social account (truthsocial.com/@chris), which Chris Pavlovski, our Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, also uses as a means for personal communications and observations. The information we post through these social media channels may be deemed material. Accordingly, investors should monitor these social media channels in addition to following our press releases, SEC filings and public conference calls and webcasts. The social media channels that we intend to use as a means of disclosing the information described above may be updated from time to time as listed on our investor relations website.

Contact: press@rumble.com

data.title: Australia's IPD Group rises on positive half-year results
data.description:
** Shares of IPD Group rise 4.5% to A$4.41

** Stock up as much as 7.8% to A$4.55 earlier in the day, their largest intraday pct gain since late August 2024

** The electrical equipment distributor logs HY NPAT from ordinary activities A$13.3 million ($8.49 million) vs A$9.5 million last year

** Says co's core business benefited from growth in industries such as data centres and water & waste water, despite challenging market conditions

** More than 199,000 shares change hands vs 30-day avg volume of 119,000 shares

** Stock up 14.5% YTD, including current session's moves

($1 = 1.5674 Australian dollars)

data.title: New Zealand Shares Fall 1.7% to Close at Lowest Since October — Market Talk
data.description: New Zealand's NZX-50 fell 1.7% to 12531.72, hitting its lowest closing level since early October amid widespread losses. The benchmark index dropped at the start of Monday's session following a weak lead from U.S. equities and remained in the red for the entire day, marking its fifth consecutive daily decline. The NZX-50 is now down 4.4% for 2025. Utility, aged-care and real-estate stocks were among the biggest losers. Mercury NZ, Contact Energy and Meridian Energy lost between 0.7% and 1.9%. None of the 25 largest stocks by market capitalization finished higher. However, lenders Westpac, Heartland and ANZ pared losses, adding between 0.7% and 1.5%. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Market Chatter: Toymaker Kayou Revives Hong Kong IPO Plans
data.description:
Kayou is reviving its plans for an initial public offering in Hong Kong, with filing to be submitted as early as February, Reuters reported Feb. 21.

The Chinese trading card and collectible toy company, which previously attempted to list in 2024 but faced regulatory hurdles, is seeking about $500 million in the IPO, according to the report.

Improved IPO approval rates have encouraged more Chinese firms, particularly in the consumer and tech sectors, to seek offshore funding, Reuters wrote. Kayou follows the success of toymaker Bloks Group, which saw the retail portion of its IPO 6,000 times oversubscribed.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Shell to Grow Interest in Operated Ursa Platform to Over 61%
data.description:
Shell agreed to acquire an additional 15.96% working interest in its operated Ursa Tension-Leg Platform in the Gulf of America from ConocoPhillips Co., according to a Feb. 21 release.

The deal, signed through its Shell Offshore and Shell Pipeline subsidiaries, will increase the British oil and gas company's stake in the project to 61.35% from 45.3884%.

Other owners of the Ursa platform are BP's (BP.L) BP Exploration & Production and ECP GOM III, with interests of 22.6916% and 15.96%, respectively.

Completion of the acquisition is expected by the end of the second quarter, subject to customary closing conditions, including regulatory approvals.

data.title: CDL Investments New Zealand Posts Higher Fiscal Year 2024 Earnings as Revenue Rises; Shares Up 5%
data.description:
CDL Investments New Zealand reported Monday full-year diluted earnings of NZ$0.0528 per share, up from NZ$0.0464 per share a year earlier.

Revenue for the 12 months to Dec. 31, 2024, was NZ$49.1 million, up 57% from NZ$30.8 million a year ago.

The board declared a dividend of NZ$0.035 per share, the same as in the previous reported period, payable on May 16 to shareholders of record as of May 2.

The residential property developer's shares were up 5% in recent trade.

data.title: S2 Resources Signs Earn-In Agreement to Assume 51% Interest in Victoria Gold Joint Venture; Shares Down 3%
data.description:
S2 Resources signed an earn-in agreement with Valkea Resources to assume its 51% interest in the Glenfine gold joint venture in Victoria by sole funding a total expenditure of AU$200,000 within four years, according to an Australian bourse filing on Monday.

The deal represents the final component of a multi-faceted deal centered on the sale of S2's Finnish assets.

S2 plans to conduct detailed relogging of a historical core at the project as well as 3D modeling to generate future targets for drilling.

The deal is subject to Valkea obtaining the TSX Venture Exchange's approval and receiving ministerial approval and registration under the Mineral Resources (Sustainable Development) Act in Victoria.

In the event of earn-in, S2 will grant Valkea a 2% net smelter return royalty, which S2 can buy back for CA$2 million at any time.

S2 Resources' shares fell almost 3% in recent trading on Monday.

data.title: JA Solar Technology Plans to List in Hong Kong
data.description:
JA Solar Technology plans to apply for listing on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Saturday.

The solar panel maker's board of directors approved the proposal, but it is still subject to regulatory approvals, the filing said.

JA Solar's shares rose more than 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Block's ASX-listed shares plunge on quarterly profit miss
data.description:
** ASX-listed shares of Block plunge as much as 13% to A$108, lowest since October 24, 2024

** Stock logs biggest intraday pct drop since March 24, 2023

** ASX stock among top losers on benchmark ASX 200

** Payments firm misses Q4 profit estimates as holiday season spending and gains from post-election surge in bitcoin lagged expectations

** About 716,985 shares traded vs the 30-day avg of 283,318

** YTD, XYZ down 23.3%
